query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Cobbler', 'A person who repairs shoes', 'The essay highlights the cobbler of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Coddle', 'To treat in an indulgent or overprotective way', 'Over time, the community learned to coddle its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Codicil', 'A document that amends a previously executed will', 'Her codicil became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Coerce', 'To persuade', 'Over time, the community learned to coerce its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Cogent', 'Convincing and logical', '“Her cogent argument persuaded the jury.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Cogitate', 'To meditate, to ponder, to think deeply', 'Over time, the community learned to cogitate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Cognate', 'Related by birth, of the same parentage', 'The author hoped the new policy would cognate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Cognizant', 'Having knowledge or awareness', 'The passage presents a cognizant portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Cohere', 'To stick together', 'Over time, the community learned to cohere its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Coherent', 'Clear and logical in thought or speech', '“His speech was well-structured and coherent.”', 'adjective', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Cohesive', 'Having unity', 'The reviewer described the tone as cohesive, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Coiffure', 'To style or arrange hair', 'Over time, the community learned to coiffure its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Coil', 'Wound into a series of loops or spirals', '“The rope was coiled neatly on the dock.”', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Coincide', 'To occur at the same time or to be in agreement', '“The holiday coincided with her birthday this year.”', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Coincidence', 'An occurence, by chance, of more than one event at one time', 'Her coincidence became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Collaborate', 'To work together with others to achieve a common goal', 'The author hoped the new policy would collaborate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Collage', 'A work of art, made from assembling different items into one', 'Scholars noted a striking collage between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Collapse', 'To fall down suddenly, to cave in', 'Over time, the community learned to collapse its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Collate', 'To gather pages in their proper sequence', 'Over time, the community learned to collate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Collateral', 'Parallel, along the same vein, side by side', 'The essay highlights the collateral of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Colleague', 'A person with whom one works with', 'Scholars noted a striking colleague between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Collective', 'Gathered into a mass, sum, or body', 'What seemed collective at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Collector', 'A person who collects, or who manages a collection', 'Her collector became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Collegian', 'A student (or a former student) of a college', 'Scholars noted a striking collegian between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Collide', 'To impact directly, especially if violent', 'Critics argued that the plan might collide the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Collier', 'A ship for carrying coal', 'Her collier became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Collision', 'An instance of violent contact between objects', 'The essay highlights the collision of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Colloquial', 'Ordinary or familiar language rather than formal language', 'Scholars noted a striking colloquial between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Colloquialism', 'An informal word or expression more suitable for speech than formal writing', 'Her colloquialism became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Colloquy', 'A conversation or dialogue', 'Her colloquy became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Collusion', 'A secret agreement, especially for treacherous purposes', 'Her collusion became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Colossus', 'A statue of gigantic size, wonders of the ancient world', 'Scholars noted a striking colossus between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Combatant', 'An armed fighter', 'Scholars noted a striking combatant between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Combustion', 'The process of burning something', 'Scholars noted a striking combustion between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Comely', '(woman) pleasant to look at, agreeable', 'She spoke comely, leaving little doubt about her position.', 'adverb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Comestible', 'Suitable to be eaten, edible', 'The author hoped the new policy would comestible tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Comical', 'Funny, amusing', 'Scholars noted a striking comical between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Commandeer', 'To seize for military use', 'The author hoped the new policy would commandeer tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Commemorate', 'To honour the memory of someone or something with a ceremony', 'Critics argued that the plan might commemorate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Commend', 'Formally praise', 'Scholars noted a striking commend between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Commensurate', 'Corresponding in size, amount, or degree', '“The salary was commensurate with her experience.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Commentary', 'A series of comments or annotations on another work', 'The essay highlights the commentary of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Commingle', 'To blend, to mix', 'The author hoped the new policy would commingle tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Commiserate', 'To feel or express compassion or sympathy for', 'Critics argued that the plan might commiserate the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Commissarial', 'A shop supplying food and equipment, as for a military camp', 'Scholars noted a striking commissarial between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Commission', 'To send or officially charge someone to do something', 'Over time, the community learned to commission its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Commitment', 'A pledge or promise', 'Scholars noted a striking commitment between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Committal', 'Of or relating to a committee', 'The author hoped the new policy would committal tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Commodious', 'Spacious and convenient, roomy and comfortable', 'What seemed commodious at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Commodity', 'A useful or valuable thing', 'Scholars noted a striking commodity between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
